title: TEN Framework: Voice AI That Can Actually Be Interrupted
summary: TEN Framework is an open-source framework for building real-time voice AI agents that handles streaming audio, interruptions, VAD, turn detection, STT, LLMs, TTS, avatars, telephony, and multimodal workflows. It uses graph-based extensions and TMAN Designer to address the production challenges of natural voice conversations, such as handling user interruptions and pauses.
writeup: This Better Stack video covers TEN Framework, an open-source framework for building real-time voice AI agents that handles streaming audio, voice activity detection (VAD), turn detection, STT, LLMs, TTS, avatars, telephony, and multimodal workflows. The focus is production challenges of natural voice conversations, specifically handling user interruptions and pauses, which separate real-time voice agents from simpler request-response pipelines. Key takeaways: TEN uses graph-based extensions to compose these components, and the TMAN Designer provides tooling for building and wiring those workflows.
